_comment: Trigger event page queue. Each entry produces ONE JSON at content/data/events/<slug>.json via coveredusa-event-writer.

events.notes: Aging off parent's plan triggers 60-day SEP. Many people don't realize they keep coverage through end of birth month before losing it. Options: marketplace SEP, employer plan if working, Medicaid if low income. Common mistake: assuming coverage ends on birthday — varies by plan.

Marriage triggers 60-day SEP under ACA. Spouse can be added to your employer plan within 30 days. Or you can both enroll in marketplace together (combined MAGI affects subsidies). Tax filing status changes (must file jointly to qualify for PTC).

Birth or adoption triggers 60-day SEP. Baby retroactively covered from birth date. Most employer plans require enrollment within 30 days. CHIP available year-round for kids. Common: pediatrician network confusion at enrollment.

Medicare IEP is 7 months: 3 before, month of 65th birthday, 3 after. Late enrollment penalties for Part B (10% per year forever) and Part D. Common: assuming auto-enrollment (only happens if already on Social Security). Spouses still working can delay Part B if covered by employer plan with 20+ employees.

Post-COVID Medicaid unwinding continues into 2026. Losing Medicaid triggers SEP for ACA marketplace. 90-day SEP per CMS (longer than standard 60-day). Many can re-enroll if income still qualifies — admin reasons drove most disenrollments. Reference KFF Medicaid unwinding tracker.

Permanent move triggers 60-day SEP. Marketplace plans are state-specific — old plan likely doesn't work in new state. Medicaid eligibility varies by state (especially relevant for non-expansion-to-expansion moves). Provider network completely changes.

Retiring before 65 means losing employer coverage but not yet eligible for Medicare. 60-day SEP. Options: marketplace, COBRA, spouse's plan. ACA subsidies based on retirement income (often lower than working income = bigger subsidy). 401k withdrawals count as MAGI.

Divorce triggers 60-day SEP for the spouse who loses dependent coverage. COBRA available for up to 36 months for divorced spouse. New household composition changes MAGI and Medicaid eligibility. Custody affects child coverage.

Going self-employed often means losing employer coverage. Loss-of-coverage SEP triggers (60 days). Self-employed health insurance deduction available for marketplace plans. HSA-qualified HDHP attractive for high-income self-employed. Estimated quarterly tax obligations affect MAGI calculation. Frame as 'you chose this transition' not 'panic'.

events.eventName: Just Graduated College (Off Student Health Plan)
events.notes: Losing student health plan triggers SEP. Options: parent's plan (until 26), employer plan if newly hired, marketplace SEP, Medicaid if low income (very common for new grads). Common: assuming you're stuck without coverage during job search.
